    Stay hungry, stay foolish.

    If not Now, When?

    Zirui Li

    PhD student @ IVRC in Beijing Institute of Technology and Visiting Researcher in TU Delft

    • Beijing, China
    • Website
    • Email
    • GitHub

    About Me

    Hello! My name is Zirui Li (李子睿). I am a PhD candidate working under the supervision of Prof. Jianwei Gong in School of Mechanical Engineering at Beijing Institute of Technology. I am also a Visiting Researcher at TU Delft under the supervision of Prof. Bart van Arem, Prof. Victor Knoop and Prof. Meng Wang.

    My research interests cover the combination of decision making and motion planning algorithms. My focus is more about interactive driver behaviour modelling and learning-based motion planning algorithms with CARLA simulation platform.

    Service

    • Reviewer for IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium (IV) 2019-2020
    • Reviewer for IEEE International Conference on Unmanned Systems (ICUS) 2020
    • Reviewer for IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Vehicles

    Research Interests

    • Interaction-aware Decision Making
    • The combination of decision making and motion planning algorithms
    • Graph Neural Network (GNN)
    • Transferable Driver Behaviour Modelling
    • Machine Learning Techniques that Improve the Performance and Efficiency of Classical Motion Planning Methods

    Open Positions

    If you are interested in doing research intern with me, please feel free to contact me by Email (3120195255@bit.edu.cn).

    News

    Oct 2020

    Our paper “Importance Weighted Gaussian Process Regression for Transferable Driver Behaviour Learning in the Lane Change Scenario” is accepted by the IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology.

    Aug 2020

    I successfully get two years CSC funding to support my visiting study in TU Delft.

    July 2020

    Two papers “Driver Behavior Modelling at the Urban Intersection via Canonical Correlation Analysis “ and “A Survey on Sensor Technologies for Unmanned Ground Vehicles “ are accepted by IEEE ICUS2020.

    May 2020

    Our papers “Prediction of Pedestrian Risky Level for Intelligent Vehicles” and “Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Combined with Motion Primitives for Automated Overtaking” are accepted by IEEE IV2020.

    May 2019

    Our paper “A Comparative Study on Transferable Driver Behavior Learning Methods in the Lane-Changing Scenario” is accepted for presentation by the The 22nd IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems.

    January 2019

    Our paper “Transferable Driver Behavior Learning via Distribution Adaption in the Lane Change Scenario” is accepted for presentation by the 2019 IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium (IV).
